nouns.summary: People describe him as despised; the text also states that those people did not consider him, implying they treated him with contempt and rejection.
Men are depicted as shunning/rejecting him; their attitude is tied to his being treated as despised and to their hiding their attention/face from him.
He is characterized as a “man” whose identity is defined by his sorrows and by being acquainted with sickness.
He is described as belonging to or characterized by sorrows, indicating that sorrows are associated with him as a defining feature.
He is described as one who is acquainted with sickness/illness, meaning his experience includes illness.
People respond to him by hiding their faces from him, showing active rejection or avoidance.
